title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: contributors maintainers project, pledge respect people contribute reporting issues, posting feature requests, updating documentation, submitting pull requests patches, activities. committed making participation project harassment-free experience everyone, regardless level experience, gender, gender identity expression, sexual orientation, disability, personal appearance, body size, race, ethnicity, age, religion. Examples unacceptable behavior participants include use sexual language imagery, derogatory comments personal attacks, trolling, public private harassment, insults, unprofessional conduct. Project maintainers right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct. Project maintainers follow Code Conduct may removed project team. Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported opening issue contacting one project maintainers. Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant (https://www.contributor-covenant.org), version 1.0.0, available https://contributor-covenant.org/version/1/0/0/.

text: ggseg 2.0 atlas package providing brain atlas data ggseg ecosystem. data-package custom functions.

title: Quick instructions for AI coding agents working on ggsegSUIT
text: data/*.rda — Unified ggseg_atlas objects (2D + 3D one object) R/data.R — Roxygen2 documentation atlas R/ggsegSUIT-package.R — Package-level docs, imports ggseg.formats data-raw/ — Scripts generated/converted atlas data

title: Quick instructions for AI coding agents working on ggsegSUIT
text: package depends ggseg.formats provides ggseg_atlas class. atlas objects work ggseg::geom_brain() (2D) ggseg3d::ggseg3d() (3D).

title: Quick instructions for AI coding agents working on ggsegSUIT
text: Update docs: devtools::document() Run tests: devtools::test() (testthat edition 3, describe/style) Full check: devtools::check() Build pkgdown site: pkgdown::build_site()

title: Quick instructions for AI coding agents working on ggsegSUIT
text: code comments except necessary workarounds Roxygen2 markdown enabled Tests use describe/BDD style vdiffr visual regression NEWS.md tracks user-facing changes (CI/pkgdown changes) URLs point ggseg GitHub org (LCBC-UiO)

text: SUIT cerebellar lobular atlas ggseg ecosystem. Diedrichsen J, Balsters JH, Flavell J, Cussans E, & Ramnani N (2009). probabilistic MR atlas human cerebellum. NeuroImage, 46(1), 39-46.

title: SUIT Cerebellar Lobular Atlas for the ggseg Ecosystem
text: recommend installing ggseg-atlases ggseg r-universe: can install package GitHub :
code: options(repos = c(   ggseg = "https://ggseg.r-universe.dev",   CRAN = "https://cloud.r-project.org" ))  install.packages("ggsegSUIT") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("ggsegverse/ggsegSUIT")

title: ggsegSUIT: SUIT Cerebellar Lobular Atlas for the 'ggseg' Ecosystem — ggsegSUIT-package
text: SUIT cerebellar parcellation anatomical lobules deep nuclei 'ggseg' ecosystem. Provides unified 'ggseg_atlas' object 2D polygon geometry 3D vertex indices, use 'ggseg' 'ggseg3d'.

title: ggsegSUIT: SUIT Cerebellar Lobular Atlas for the 'ggseg' Ecosystem — ggsegSUIT-package
SUIT Cerebellar Lobular Atlas — suit
text: Cerebellar parcellation anatomical lobules deep nuclei SUIT atlas (Diedrichsen et al., 2009). Contains 2D polygon geometry 3D meshes.

title: SUIT Cerebellar Lobular Atlas — suit
code: suit() #>  #> ── suit ggseg atlas ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Type: cerebellar #> Regions: 13 #> Hemispheres: left, right, vermis #> Views: flatmap, nuclei #> Palette: ✔ #> Rendering: ✔ ggseg #> ✔ ggseg3d (meshes) #> ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> # A tibble: 34 × 3 #>    hemi   region     label            #>    <chr>  <chr>      <chr>            #>  1 left   I_IV       left_I_IV        #>  2 right  I_IV       right_I_IV       #>  3 left   V          left_V           #>  4 right  V          right_V          #>  5 left   VI         left_VI          #>  6 vermis VI         vermis_VI        #>  7 right  VI         right_VI         #>  8 left   CrusI      left_CrusI       #>  9 vermis CrusI      vermis_CrusI     #> 10 right  CrusI      right_CrusI      #> 11 left   CrusII     left_CrusII      #> 12 vermis CrusII     vermis_CrusII    #> 13 right  CrusII     right_CrusII     #> 14 left   VIIb       left_VIIb        #> 15 vermis VIIb       vermis_VIIb      #> 16 right  VIIb       right_VIIb       #> 17 left   VIIIa      left_VIIIa       #> 18 vermis VIIIa      vermis_VIIIa     #> 19 right  VIIIa      right_VIIIa      #> 20 left   VIIIb      left_VIIIb       #> 21 vermis VIIIb      vermis_VIIIb     #> 22 right  VIIIb      right_VIIIb      #> 23 left   IX         left_IX          #> 24 vermis IX         vermis_IX        #> 25 right  IX         right_IX         #> 26 left   X          left_X           #> 27 vermis X          vermis_X         #> 28 right  X          right_X          #> 29 left   Dentate    left_Dentate     #> 30 right  Dentate    right_Dentate    #> 31 left   Interposed left_Interposed  #> 32 right  Interposed right_Interposed #> 33 left   Fastigial  left_Fastigial   #> 34 right  Fastigial  right_Fastigial

title: ggsegSUIT 0.0.1
text: Cerebellar atlas type using SUIT flatmap projection Anatomical labels hemisphere prefixes (left_, right_, vermis_) Deep cerebellar nuclei (Dentate, Interposed, Fastigial) separate 3D meshes Atlas accessed via suit() function
